headline: National Flood Warning System Covers Only 55% of Rivers
context: DID national flood warning system covers only 55% of monitored river basins with 45% relying on manual gauge reading.

cards.big: Flood warning covers only 55% of river basins with automated monitoring
cards.sub: 45% still rely on manual gauge reading causing 2-4 hour warning delays

cards.big: Automated stations number 1,200 but 2,200 needed for full coverage
cards.sub: 280 existing stations were non-functional for more than 30 days in 2025

cards.big: December 2025 Pahang floods caught 45,000 residents without warning
cards.sub: Sensor failure in upstream monitoring station delayed alerts by 6 hours

cards.big: System upgrade allocated RM480 million but only RM180 million disbursed
cards.sub: Full nationwide coverage projected for 2030 at current implementation pace

cards.big: Warning gaps test whether disaster preparedness matches the frequency of flooding events
55% river coverage for flood warnings. The unmonitored 45% is where the next disaster begins.
